title: tests: fix diff cmd test on macOS HFS+, fixes #8860
body: If we touch too quickly after file creation, it would not noticably update the timestamp.

step_plan.summary: Make the failing diff command test deterministic on macOS HFS+ by replacing a fragile immediate Path.touch() call with platform-aware logic that advances the file's mtime by >1 second (using os.utime on Darwin), avoiding sleeps while ensuring the timestamp change is observed.

steps.operation: Replace Path('input/file_touched').touch() with a macOS-aware utime that advances mtime by >1s (and add os import if needed)
steps.reason: On macOS HFS+ timestamps have 1s granularity; touching immediately after file creation can leave the recorded mtime unchanged and cause the test's timestamp-only change assertion to flake. Explicitly advancing the file's mtime (old_mtime + 2s) when running on Darwin ensures the filesystem-rounding yields a different timestamp deterministically without adding sleeps or changing test semantics on other platforms.
steps.side_effects: Only test code is changed; production code unaffected. Tests will be deterministic on macOS HFS+ and unchanged on other platforms. Implementation will likely require adding an 'import os' at the top of the test file (and use the existing is_darwin flag if present; if not present the change may add a small platform check like sys.platform == 'darwin'). This change may alter ctime on POSIX (expected by the test), and has no API implications. Downstream tests that depend on an unchanged touch() side-effect are unlikely, but if any test relies on exact atime/mtime behavior beyond this test it should be reviewed. No change to test runtime performance (no sleeps introduced).
